pattern: Treat rooms as nodes and the keys found in a room as outgoing edges, then a single traversal from room zero answers whether every node is reachable.
hints: Each room hands you keys to other rooms, which is exactly a directed-edge relationship between rooms.
Start at room zero and explore: every time you enter a new room, queue up the rooms its keys unlock.
Run a depth-first or breadth-first search from room zero, marking rooms visited; afterward check whether the visited count equals the number of rooms.

edge_inputs.description: a simple chain unlocks every room
a room's only key is locked inside itself
two rooms where the first opens the second
two rooms but no key to the second
self-referencing keys still leave a room unreachable
duplicate keys across rooms, all reachable
a cycle that never reaches the last room

stress.description: random key sets; a spanning chain is always seeded so reachability varies but stays solvable to compute quickly
stress.note: mix of fully-reachable and partially-reachable layouts
